programme.desc: An intruder raids a social clubs safe while the manager is on the phone, a burglar tries to rob a house but meets his match at the foot of the stairs; and a man breaks into a garage and ends up bathing in engine oil.

programme.title: Africas Hunters
programme.desc: A clan of spotted hyenas moves into a den close to the litter of wild dog pups.

programme.title: Africas Claws and Jaws
programme.desc: Sharks are among the planets oldest and most highly evolved predators. Along southern Africas coast, pioneers dedicate their lives to learning more about these creatures, and spreading awareness about their plight.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs. Pride
programme.desc: The Kakumbi see a leopard whos been stalking them, Julz goes fishing with Simon, explores mealtime pack hierarchy and witnesses a heartbreaking vigil for a badly injured Kakumbi pup.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs. Pride
programme.desc: Heading back to the den with the Kakumbi who are mourning the loss of two pups. A look at a hyena den and lion pride trying to take down a buffalo and catching up with the Manzi who are in a standoff with a leopard and a herd of angry elephants.

programme.title: Dogs: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee follows a pack of African hunting dogs and meets a fox with ears longer than its legs. Plus, a jackals cunning is caught on camera.

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Counts down some of the most incredible, spectacular and hilarious real life crashes in the world. Video cameras capture massive pile ups, road rage and misguided overtaking manoeuvres.

programme.title: A Fistful of Dollars
programme.desc: Sergio Leones classic about a mysterious drifters involvement with two families, the Baxters and the Rojos, in a Mexican border town. Instead of leaving town, the drifter gets caught up in the feud and plays both sides of the warring factions and gets rich in the bargain.

programme.title: Clint Eastwood: A Life in Film
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood, the legendary actor and Academy Award-winning director of films including Unforgiven and Million Dollar Baby, talks candidly about his extraordinary life and career.

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam

programme.title: Killer In My Village
programme.desc: After a man is locked in a storeroom in a burning building, the race is on to identify those who started the blaze. But a closer look uncovers bigger issues.

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: A closer look at New Zealand cops as they deal with the challenges of monitoring the nations busiest motorway.

programme.title: Wild Dogs
programme.desc: Discover the ways in which natural selection and human pressure have shaped the raccoon dog, the golden jackal and the maned wolf.

programme.title: Kingdom Of The Tides
programme.desc: From the untamed Pacific to the wild Bay of Fundy, Cullis-Suzuki embarks on a coast-to-coast adventure to discover the survival secrets of those that inhabit Canadas intertidal zones.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Meet the unlikely collaborators working to ensure mutual survival in the Kalahari Basin. Learn of an elephant clans symbiotic link to fruiting anaboom trees, a meerkats alliance with a feisty drongo bird, and more flora-and-fauna odd couples.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Thirst for rain alongside the animals of the Kalahari as elephant herds cooperate and meerkats adopt a strange babysitter in the depths of a brutally dry year.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Track scientists who have arrived in the Kalahari to test a hypothesis: that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the regions species. Observe as they monitor behaviour, decipher data, and deliver fascinating conclusions.

programme.title: My Best Friends An Animal
programme.desc: Rocco, Harold and Cindy: Follow Shanet, who spends her days tending to the needs of Harold Custard, the penguin and feel the love as Barista cares for Cindy the baboon.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The ruthless realm of some of the stealthiest and cleverest insects in the animal kingdom -- from praying mantises and aquatic insects to dragonflies.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The crucial ecological roles that beetles play in nearly every environment, and why they are often regarded "the most important species on the planet."

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Drought
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered its winter season. Predator and prey are forced to live in close proximity due to a limited supply of water.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Flood
programme.desc: Waters flood Southern Floridas mangroves and marshes. Many of the 600 animal species that live in the Everglades disperse during this time, expanding their hunting and breeding grounds.

programme.title: Attenborough At 90: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: In celebration of Sir David Attenboroughs 90th Birthday, this intimate film presents new interviews, eye-opening behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips from some of his most recent films.

programme.title: A David Attenborough Special
programme.desc: On his 89th birthday, David Attenborough talks about his career, his recent record-breaking dive on the Great Barrier Reef and issues facing the natural world today.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: From turf wars, to food fights, to mating mayhem, nature is all about competition and the quest to be the best. Every day, the planet is a battleground as animals go head to head with their own kind.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: From turf wars, to food fights, to mating mayhem, nature is all about competition and the quest to be the best. Every day, the planet is a battleground as animals go head to head with their own kind.

programme.title: Super Shark Highway
programme.desc: Discover shark community hierarchies as the northern team studies how young blacktips learn social and survival skills. Plus, a look at Rainbow Beach with the southern team, who witness the sand tiger sharks most elusive behaviour: mating.

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee comes within a whisker of some of Earths most majestic predators as he embarks on a remarkable journey through the feline family tree.

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family

programme.title: Lord Jim
programme.desc: Powerful tale of cowardice and redemption, based on Joseph Conrads classic novel. During a hurricane, a seaman abandons his ship and its passengers to save himself. Many die horribly, and the sailor knows that he will always be branded a coward. Much later, when he takes on a job transporting dynamite upriver to a tribe rebelling against oppression, he hopes that the perilous task will lead to spiritual regeneration.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Archaeologists Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn and historian Ruth attempt to bring Morwellham Quay in Devon back to life as it was in its Edwardian heyday. They begin setting up home in one of the cottages, where the first task is unblocking the chimney so they can get the range working. They prepare for the arrival of their first livestock, and Ruth cooks a sheeps head stew.

programme.desc: Amid Belfasts buzzy food scene, Rick tucks into Filipino-style Sunday lunch. On Northern Irelands Atlantic coast, he meets an award-winning chef with a no waste approach who turns fish offal and species such as dog fish into stunning dishes.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: In October, the team branch out into market gardening, all-year-round egg production and beef cattle. They begin training the shire horses for a year of work in the fields. They take on a pair of goats who prove to be more than a handful at milking time, and Ruth preserves supplies for the winter. Peter visits a cooper and learns how to make a barrel, and Alex launches a chicken enterprise.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: In Bury, at Britains best market, Rick immerses himself in the rich food traditions of north west England. In Manchester, he meets Joe Otway, an exciting chef reinventing those traditions for a hip crowd.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team present the makeover show, transforming unloved spaces into dream gardens for some very special people. In this episode, Alan and the team create a space-age garden in rural Cornwall for Chris Finney and his family. In 2003, Chris became the youngest serviceman ever to receive the George Cross, awarded for his extraordinary courage under fire, but since leaving the forces he has not always found civilian life easy.

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, top trader Melissa Downhill and luxury upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Chipping Campden to find bargains they can flip into profit. The three experts have just one day to scour its flea markets and scrap yards.

programme.title: Highclere: Behind the Scenes
programme.desc: Its Christmas, and we go behind the scenes as the castle and grounds are transformed into a winter wonderland. The team wrestle their biggest-ever tree, and butler Louis gets merry hosting a festive cocktail master class.

programme.title: Richard Wilsons Highland Fling
programme.desc: After almost 60 years of London life, Richard Wilson, one of Britains most loved actors, fulfils a lifelong ambition to explore the Highlands and Islands of his native country.

programme.desc: Seasoned floating home couple Pat and Sue Willan plan to buy their very last boat and opt for a state-of-the-art, all-mod-cons Dutch barge. They have a budget of over £400,000 and plans for very contemporary kitchen fittings.

programme.desc: Sue Perkins and Liza Tarbuck drive the legendary Ho Chi Minh Trail, a network of dangerous roads hacked through mountains and jungles of Laos and Vietnam. Sue and Liza face a gruelling drive to some of the least explored sections of the trail.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In the Scottish capital of Edinburgh, hidden from view, a series of vaults tell a murky story of opportunity, crime and destitution. programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

programme.desc: Louis struggles to come to terms with the infamous Phelps family from Topeka, Kansas who picket the funerals of dead soldiers killed in Iraq as a protest against an America that tolerates homosexuality. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team head to Suffolk to Helmingham Hall that has been home to twenty generations of the Tollemache family. The moated Tudor manor house, with two working draw bridges, is the perfect backdrop for a roadshow brimming with antique finds. Lee Young discovers a collection of seventeenth and eighteenth century decorative boxes that the owner is using as his pension fund. Militaria specialist Bill Harriman is intrigued by a mediaeval sword dredged up from a pond on a golf course.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team make a return visit to the Black Country Living Museum in the West Midlands on one of the hottest days of the year. Susan Rumfitt discovers some jewels fit for a princess, while Hilary Kay explores local motor racing history at the Sunbeam Motor Car Company. A surrealist picture appeals to Rupert Maas but is it the real deal? Plus, the show takes a surprising turn to the Wild West with one of the rarest guns Bill Harriman has ever seen: a Colt 37 revolver!
